reviews.review_text:
Don't normalize external launchers, stop cluttering my disk with useless crap and horrible broken external launchers.
Just launch my game.

"Battlefield V" despite all the criticism it received over the years is still for me one of the best multiplayer war games ever created. Once again I am focusing mostly on the multiplayer because that is where Battlefield always delivered its best moments. Battlefield V captured that feeling of large scale war incredibly well, the explosions, constant chaos, planes flying overhead, tanks pushing through destroyed buildings and bullets flying everywhere around your teammates made every match feel intense and cinematic. The graphics and sound design were absolutely beautiful and together created moments that honestly felt like scenes straight out of a war movie. What I miss the most in modern shooters is exactly this kind of atmosphere, because back then games still tried to immerse you in war instead of constantly selling flashy colorful skins that completely destroy immersion. Battlefield V despite its flaws still kept a lot of that raw battlefield feeling where the focus was on the atmosphere, teamwork and the chaos of war itself. For me Battlefield 1 and Battlefield V are still two of the greatest multiplayer war games ever made because they understood how important immersion and atmosphere really are.
The game completely freezes sometimes mid gameplay for no reason. I did everything against it from Reddit to Google and nothing helped. Game in general is fun, but the freezes kill the vibe.

this game is not as good as battlefield 1, the gameplay is a lot more modern fps shooter-like with fast TTK and op run and gun SMGs. there are less players than battlefield 1, however still plenty enough to find games. vehicles spawn a lot and they are very OP. 
some of the worst aspects of this game is the fact that there is a lack of factions, there is only Germany, USA, Japan, and UK, almost every ww2 game should have the russian faction since they have so many iconic weapons and vehicles, however this is because support was cut short for this game i think. 
theres also random weird skins and women soldiers that ruin the immersion, if there was a soviet faction then women soldiers would make sense, but having german women and japanese women as soldiers is crazy since they were ultra conservative countries that didn't want women fighting in war.
finally, there arent many weapons and equipment in the game.
overall, its an average ww2 shooter, i still play it sometimes since its the only battlefield ww2 fps game, its the third playable battlefield behind bf1 and bf6.

Just started playing the new Battlefield recently. The graphics and large-scale battles are pretty cool, but after a while the gameplay can feel kinda repetitive sometimes. Still fun with friends though, especially during chaotic matches.
This games doesnt open. When it opens it lags a lot. This is the worst game I played so far. Do not buy it!!!!!

This game is one big joke.
Apparently, the former DICE director was inspired by the words of his 13-year-old daughter who plays Fortnite.
Lies and historical fables.
(For example, playing with weapons and vehicles that don't exist yet.)
Enemy soldier models in winter uniforms in the desert, etc.
And as Americans, we shoot with a Kar98.
I don't recommend it.

It's ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ bad. It's just bad. I don't even know how to describe it. If you want to stutter along, congrats, here's your ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game. It has zero shader installer, so you'll stutter around every multiplayer match like a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥♥♥. That carries over to campaign, and that's even better. What a completely anachronistic butchering of history this game is. Every ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ aspect. Weapons are raped, vehicles are raped, and the character designs are raped. literally one of the worst AAA games I've ever ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ played, and the multiplayer does not count as some sort of retribution. You suck Dice. You suck, EA sucks, all of you suck. Grow a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ pair and release a game that doesn't suck complete ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ balls.

This game does not come close to BF1 in immersion but it has its strong points, like weapon trees and and the large variety of weapons (the latter something BF1 also has). Overall, solid. BUT, whenever I play BF5, I am just constantly reminded of BF1.

♥♥♥♥ EA and ♥♥♥♥ that launcher, I bought Battlefield V and 1 because they were on sale and thought maybe the campaign isn't that bad, but to play the campaign you have to go through dozens of offers, the launcher keeps crashing, I uninstalled Battlefield V and their ♥♥♥♥♥♥ launcher and I'll probably never install them again.

BFV's campaign is pretty good, but also very disappointing. 

The stories are pretty good, even solid but inferior to BF1 in almost every sense. How they feel they could get away from a WWII game without a chapter in the Eastern Front showing the USSR/Third Reich is UNFATHOMABLE for me. That battle was the one that defined the war, and it's not even mentioned here. I can't even call this American propaganda, because the Americans are also not present… It just feels like a missed opportunity to portray WWII like this. I know the whole point of BF's War Stories is to shine a light on unknown stories from WWII, for example, the Tirailleur story that was VERY GOOD and a highlight of this campaign, but not a single story in the Eastern Front is insane. I am also very surprised to see The Last Tiger chapter, it was bold, depressing, bleak, and brutal. Probably the best from BFV and the most relevant.

I also feel that technically the game took a big downgrade, the game feels sluggish, the explosions don't register well, the destruction is less impressive, the game stutters even in cutscenes, and strangely, I feel the graphics and art style are worse than previous games.

Soundtrack, scenarios and weapon variety are still excellent. I also loved the new mechanics (Skying anyone?).

BFV is ok, it could be better, and seeing so many downgrades, both technically and narratively, clearly shows that this was rushed out of the door incomplete. Classic EA move. 

Good enough for a recommendation, but not enough to challenge BF1's excellence.
This game is really good. The Last Tiger was my favorite campaign. This game also runs great on older video cards. I do think the multiplayer needs improving. But overall, I would recommend.
The game can't run on a rig that runs Cyberpunk on the highest settings. Countless fixes and it still has poor graphic performance and randomly crashes. Waste of my time and money.
Story in the game is amazing, sound, pacing everything is perfect
Its a really fun game and it mixes historical accuracy and accuracy in the warfare, but obviously its not perfect and has some inacuracies but its a video game

Battlefield™ V - Review

Starting Summary: EA is without question the single worst game compony ever so get this game on a deep, deep sale if at all, IMO.

The Campaign: At times its very Tired, Lazy Snowflake Writing pandering to a “Reimagined” World, DICE cannot decide whether  “History” is important or not at all important, DICE butchered History just lined it up and bang tossed it into a shallow grave kicked a little dirt on it then drove off.

The Soundtrack: It's got one.

The Graphics: Battlefield 1 looked better IMO, but to be fair some of the scenes look very nice.

The World: Maybe the Worst Version of WW2 I've ever seen, IMO.

The Characters: Felt Lazy, IMO.

The Gameplay: Kind of weird, and the guns feel off like they're shooting blanks or half powered rounds or something IDK just felt off to me.

The Multiplayer Gameplay: its okay, they have four playable factions UK, US, Japan and Germany which is weird because in the Story there is no Japan or US.
Unlike the older Battlefield Games where classes are sort of fixed on weapons its basically a free for all so everyone is COD running around with a Sten or Thompson which is annoying.
There are a lot of game modes but people only ever play the one really which again is a little annoying.
The Community was very Nice at least what I saw of it.
One of the game modes that I found the most fun was Firestorm which is like a Battle Royale which as stated before I found it very fun sadly there are very few if any people playing this game mode which again is sad.

What I Liked: I liked the very, very slight character customization.
The Last Tiger is the best Story by far IDK what happened with DICE where they somehow managed to make a good Story for this game The Last Tiger and maybe the Norway Story are worth buying the game for IMO.
Mark Strong is the Story Narrator which is great.

What I Hated: As stated before DICE butchered History just lined it up and bang tossed it into a shallow grave kicked a little dirt on it then drove off.
I also got very long load screens.
They teased us with a flying mission in the Prologue but there is not an actual flying mission which is sad.
They pansied out and didn't give the Germans their WW2 flag not even a close one not even in the campaigns which is weird because they say at the start of the Norwegian Story that “Europe Falls under the shadow of the $w@$tlk@” but I saw ZERO $w@$tlk@s so IDK DICE didn't see any.
It felt like DICE didn't know if they were telling a tragedy or a comedy its like “Hahaha, but WW2 was sad, Hahaha but please remember WW2 was sad, Hahaha.”

Bugs and Glitches: Once when exiting a match the game bugged out and I had to force stop the Game.
Once when Exiting a Story Mission the game bugged out and I had to force stop the Game.

In Closing: Its sort of sad maybe a 4/10 if I'm being honest at times it has highs of maybe 4½/10 but again I'll be honest the game rarely goes up that high and when it does its the Multiplayer not the game which is sad because I really wanted to love this game but I only sort of like it, IMO.

Battlefield V is a very good and charismatic game... from far away, that is.
My friends have been recommending it as one of the best fps shooters they have ever played, and I've been eyeing the steam page for a while, so my expectations were high. Bought it on sale, started playing right away. For me, it was anything but enjoyable. Here be my honest opinion on this game:

Pros:
1. The environment and graphics are gorgeous, especially the Pacific maps. Visual effects are realistic and well done too. Cosmetics are not over-the-top either, just decorative enough to stay believable.
2. The sound design is immersive, even though sometimes I CANNOT for the love of me hear planes coming, many times I have died because of this. 
3. Gunplay is satisfying. The weapons have weight to them, and every shot feels significant, especially with snipers. 
4. The movement is fluid and easy to get used to, although there were some issues I ran into, like the slide mechanic being a bit clunky sometimes. Other than that, running around was very fun!

Cons:
(Brace yourselves, long tangent incoming)
1. Lots of people have said this before me, but there are SO. MANY. CHEATERS. Some matches are totally unplayable! I have been shot through walls, terrain, tanks, even when I'm completely in cover they still manage to find me. And there's nothing we can do about it. There isn't even a report button!
2. Tanks are waaaaay too overpowered for how easy they are to use. Smaller maps get fully dominated by just a few of them. Planes on the other hand are the polar opposite. I found the controls hard to learn, and not intuitive at all. 
3. Matchmaking is... well, it's matchmaking! First time I joined combat I was instantly met with lvl 20 everything. Of course, these players absolutely ratted on me, which didn't make it an enjoyable experience. There are also times, when it puts me into a game that's already started. Most of the times the match's fate is already decided when I spawn in, so I can't contribute in any meaningful way.
4. I'm very torn on the map design aspect. I recognize, that most of the maps are scenic and elegantly crafted, but I could not grow to love them. There are no cold zones, where there's less action, you know, to just take a breather. Maps are very chaotic, which makes the already hard positioning even harder to understand. It feels like it constantly punishes you for every small bad decision. I don't think they're balanced either. It feels like some maps just cater to one team way more; there's obvious balance issues present with the maps. Which is a shame, because they are genuinely very pretty maps.
5. When I first started playing I encountered a bug, where the game, on every launch, kept replaying the intro sequence. I could fix it, but only by following some thousand year old Reddit prophecy. It's 2026, I should not need to become a hacker, to simply play a mediocre game, now come on now.
6. The story mode is incredibly underwhelming. We follow the life of some randos, as they have their main character moments. With plot armor of course. That was done in about 2-3 hours. As i understand that was supposed to be a kind of tutorial, but I felt like it didn't show me much of the combat features. It was more of a power trip fantasy wet dream, than a tutorial. Also, why would they choose a random inmate for a very important mission? lol

All in all Battlefield V is a game that could have. The gorgeous environment and satisfying gunplay really showed potential, that was wasted on some smelly hackers. It's a 'fine' war simulator - simulator is very strong word-, but that's all it has to offer. In my eyes it's just another abandoned multiplayer shooter, that's built on rage culture and the philosophy of "just one more match bro". I can't recommend it with clear conscience.

The story mode in Battlefield V was really enjoyable and had many great emotional moments. I liked the different war stories and characters, which made the experience feel more personal. My only complaint is that I wish it was longer, because some parts felt too short. Overall, it was a good and emotional story mode that could have had more content.

Infantry movement and gunplay: 10/10 (The pinnacle of the Battlefield series so far, prone sprinting and aiming feel unmatched)  
Battlefield atmosphere and sound: 9.5/10 (The destructive power of the Frostbite engine, gunfire, and soldiers' screams fully immerse you in war)  
Map and mode design: 8.5/10 (The Pacific map is godly, but some launch maps are mediocre)  
Vehicle balance and counterplay: 6.5/10 (Vehicles feel good, but infantry countermeasures are still painful, with rampant "potato picking")  
Operations, updates, and completeness: 4.0/10 (Marketing disaster, forcibly cut short)

This game can be fun in campaign, but if you wanna play multiplayer - be prepared for one of the most unfun experiences of your life. For some reason my team doesn't wanna revive me even if its clear. In every game there's people just crouching everywhere and sniping you from across the map somehow (I know the crouching part is a way of playing but it can get REALLY annoying). Plane control is pretty hard to get used to, and always on my team our planes get shot down every second, and no one on my team just cares about enemy team's planes. Vehicles are pretty fun to drive because they can fly up, but I don't like the fact that you can barely ram trough most of the obstacles and the guns on vehicles are not so satisfying to use. Also be ready to be sniped by tanks from across the map too. Graphics - really good, every effect, explosion, animation is well detailed and optimized so I can play with good graphics even on a weak PC. Overall I enjoy playing this just like any other Battlefield part.

I’m reviewing Battlefield V purely from the perspective of its single-player campaign.

The story is interestingly told, with different perspectives on war and different styles of combat, which kept the experience fresh. I can honestly say I enjoyed playing through it, even though I finished it fairly quickly.

There were a few moments where certain parts of the story irritated me, mostly because of the linear design that sometimes limits the way you want to approach a situation or build your own strategy. Still, it was nothing serious enough to strongly criticize.

Overall, the campaign is memorable, enjoyable, and leaves a good feeling once it is finished.
In my opinion, the best battlefield game. Haven't played it in a bit though so things may have changed.

Absolutely loved it. The campaign especially the last mission (The last tiger) was absolutely legendary. I also played the multiplayer conquest for a bit and that was fun as well.

Battlefield V honestly surprised me with how human its campaign felt. Instead of trying to make every mission feel like some over-the-top action movie, the game focuses more on the people inside the war — scared soldiers, impossible choices, sacrifice, and survival. The “War Stories” format was one of my favorite parts because each story felt personal and different, showing smaller perspectives of World War II that most games usually ignore. The mission “The Last Tiger” especially stood out to me. It doesn’t just throw endless action at you — it actually makes you think about the cost of war and what the soldiers were feeling near the end of everything. The characters felt believable, and there were moments where the game became quiet and emotional instead of just loud explosions. That balance made the campaign way more memorable for me than I expected. Visually, the campaign is beautiful. The environments, lighting, weather, and sound design make every location feel alive and immersive. Sometimes I would stop for a second just to look around because the game genuinely looks cinematic. The soundtrack also adds a lot emotionally, especially during slower scenes. The gameplay itself is smooth and satisfying. Gunplay feels weighty, movement is fluid, and the combat can become really intense without feeling unrealistic. Some missions allow stealth approaches too, which helped make the campaign feel less repetitive. Of course, the campaign isn’t perfect. Some stories are very short, and just when you start getting attached to certain characters, the mission ends. I also wish there were more War Stories because the ones included had a lot of potential. But even with that, Battlefield V gave me moments that felt emotional, immersive, and genuinely memorable. To me, Battlefield V is at its best when it stops trying to just be a shooter and instead shows the human side of war. That’s what made the campaign stick with me long after I finished it.

Each iteration, presumably because later generations weren't disciplined or learned patience, results in games where fewer and fewer people actually PTFO. End result? Lots of unbalanced games - perhaps 9/10 I play are terribly unbalanced. Almost every game is a TDM. You can thank the world of instant gratification for that; COD, Fortnite, social media - it has all bled into team based games such as this, and the itchiness of people's fingers to chase kills and deaths ruins the experience. I said years ago EA/DICE should have ditched Kills / Deaths in the scoreboard for objective based modes... but here we are.

Most of the game modes are just flavours of Conquest or Rush. Medics get smoke but support doesn't? Hmm.

Takes ages to launch thanks to a third party launcher requirement and EA Javelin.

Worth the £2.24, was never worth the full price.

Oh and the game is rammed with microtransactions - perfect for the little ones who have no impusle control.

Requires a lot of bloatware to run and even then it will only run if I have internet while booting it up and then disable the internet right before it finishes starting. If I am offline in the first place it can not connect its anti cheat software and will not boot up; if I leave the internet on when in the game it flags some software of mine which I can not turn off as cheating and shuts down the game. Consequently online doesn't work for me at all. I haven't finished the single player as of yet but it feels extremely insubstantial and not worth the cost and huge hassle.

After the enormous success of Battlefield 1, expectations for Battlefield V were exceptionally high. DICE returned to World War II with promises of squad-focused gameplay, improved destruction systems, and a live-service structure designed to evolve over time. What emerged was a technically impressive shooter that often feels caught between authenticity, blockbuster spectacle, and modern live-service trends.

At its core, Battlefield V delivers some of the strongest gameplay mechanics in the franchise. Gunplay feels sharp, movement is fluid, and combat has an immediacy that makes firefights intensely satisfying. Sliding, crouch sprinting, fortification building, and improved squad revives give the gameplay a tactical layer missing from several previous entries. Even critics who disliked the overall package frequently praised the combat systems themselves.

Visually, the game is outstanding. Maps like Rotterdam, Narvik, and the Pacific expansion environments showcase exceptional lighting, particle effects, and environmental detail. Explosions throw dirt and debris across the battlefield while tracer fire cuts through smoke-filled streets in ways that remain impressive years later. Community discussions still regularly praise the game’s fidelity and sound design.

The multiplayer is where Battlefield V performs best. Squad play feels more important than before thanks to attrition mechanics that limit ammunition and health availability. The fortification system also adds a unique strategic element, allowing players to rebuild defenses and reshape combat zones mid-match. Objective-based modes especially benefit from these additions.

However, the game constantly struggles with atmosphere and tone. Where Battlefield 1 felt grim, desperate, and emotionally immersive, Battlefield V often feels cleaner and less grounded. Many players criticized the brighter visual direction and inconsistent artistic identity, arguing that it weakened the sense of participating in World War II warfare. Reddit discussions over the years repeatedly compare the two games, with many players agreeing that Battlefield V has superior mechanics but weaker immersion.

The single-player “War Stories” campaign is similarly uneven. Certain missions, particularly The Last Tiger, stand out with emotional storytelling and strong presentation, but the overall campaign lacks the impact and cohesion of its predecessor. Critics frequently noted that stealth mechanics feel awkward and that several stories end before becoming truly compelling.

Launch issues also damaged the game’s reputation significantly. Missing content, bugs, controversial customization decisions, and slow post-launch support created frustration throughout the community. For many players, the game felt unfinished during its early months.

Over time, though, the game improved substantially. The Pacific expansion in particular is often viewed as the moment Battlefield V finally realized its potential. New maps, weapons, and vehicles injected much-needed energy into the experience, and many fans now look back on the final version of the game far more positively than they did at launch.

What makes Battlefield V fascinating is that it succeeds and fails in equal measure. Mechanically, it may be one of the smoothest and most refined Battlefield games ever made. Artistically and tonally, however, it never fully commits to a clear identity. The result is a shooter that feels exceptional moment-to-moment, even while falling short of the emotional impact associated with the series’ best entries.

Today, Battlefield V stands as an underrated but flawed chapter in the franchise, a game remembered as much for its missed potential as for its excellent gameplay.

Score: 8.1/10

It's not worth all the hype and the money.

It's just a bit boring -- creep around, shoot a bit, get shot, creep around some more. Get shot. 
Played through the first single player Africa mission in the hope that the next one (Norway) might be a bit more open and less creeping around - no - again, you alone vs a whole load of enemies. 

And then there are the bugs -- first of all, had to disable ambient occlusion or the game would just freeze randomly, taking down the whole system. Occasionally, guns cant be selected until you drop it and pick it up again. Microstutters from time to time (usually early in levels). Pretty long load times too.

The cut scenes are fine and I quite like a game to have a story - bought this cheaply on sale, but I think I've probably had enough of it now 6hrs in -- just bored of it.
